admin.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress admin --help` for exact arguments and options.

health-report.help: Emit a structured WordPress maintenance health report.

plugins.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress admin plugins --help` for exact arguments and options.

activate.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress admin plugins activate --help` for exact arguments and options.

deactivate.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress admin plugins deactivate --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete (uninstall) a WordPress plugin. Must be inactive first.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress admin plugins delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific WordPress plugin.
get.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress admin plugins get --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: plugin
arguments.help: Plugin path, slug, textdomain, or exact name from plugins list

install.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress admin plugins install --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress admin plugins list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of plugins to return

upgrade.help: Upgrade a plugin to the latest version through Jetpack's native updater.
upgrade.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress admin plugins upgrade --help` for exact arguments and options.

security-scan.help: Scan installed plugins and themes for known vulnerabilities.

options.name: --active-only
options.help: Scan only active plugins plus all themes

options.name: --table
options.help: Display affected components as a table

file-push.help: Push a local file to a remote WordPress theme through SSH.

arguments.name: remote_file
arguments.help: Relative destination path inside the theme

options.name: --backup
options.help: Back up the existing remote file before overwrite

options.name: --yes
options.help: Upload and overwrite the remote file

get.help: Get details for a specific WordPress theme.

arguments.name: theme
arguments.help: Theme stylesheet, textdomain, or exact name from themes list

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of themes to return

auth.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress auth --help` for exact arguments and options.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress auth login --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress auth logout --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.
status.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress auth status --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

add-page.help: Add a WordPress page to a navigation menu.

items.help: List items in a WordPress navigation menu.

org.usage_instructions: Use `wordpress org token` commands for WordPress.com OAuth credentials and Jetpack plugin-update tokens.

token.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress org token` to acquire and save `WPCOM_ACCESS_TOKEN` using saved WordPress.com credentials, or pass all credential options directly.

save-credential.help: Save the WordPress.com credential bundle used to acquire Jetpack tokens.
save-credential.usage_instructions: Run `wordpress org token save-credential --client-id ... --client-secret ... --username ... --password ... --site ...` to save the WordPress.com credential bundle without requesting a token.

status.help: Report whether the active profile is ready for WordPress.com plugin updates.
